intro: Brachycorythis helferi, commonly known as Helfer's Brachycorythis, is a species of orchid native to tropical Africa. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Brachycorythis helferi include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Brachycorythis helferi is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used in the form of a tincture or extract, to take advantage of its therapeutic potential.
